name: College And University
description_short: Chicago-based credit union established in 1967 offering member loans, share accounts, and financial services with NCUA insurance protection up to $250,000.
description_long:
College and University Credit Union was founded in 1967 as a member-owned, not-for-profit financial cooperative serving Chicago's union and college-affiliated communities. The institution is headquartered at 1901 West Carroll Avenue in Chicago, Illinois, and operates as an NCUA-insured credit union, providing federally-backed deposit protection. The credit union has a documented history of supporting members through five major work stoppages, positioning itself as a union-friendly financial institution committed to member welfare during economic hardship.

The credit union offers personal loans for debt consolidation, emergency expenses, and home improvement projects, along with standard share savings accounts and share certificates. Members can access loan applications, membership applications, and payroll deduction forms through their website. The institution emphasizes quick and easy loan processing for members facing unexpected financial needs or planning capital projects. Current rates and specific loan terms are available on their website, though detailed product specifications are limited in public-facing materials.

College and University Credit Union distinguishes itself through its explicit commitment to union members and its 57-year operational history serving the same community. The organization's emphasis on supporting members during labor disputes reflects a mission-driven approach uncommon among larger financial institutions. Their limited hours (Monday and Thursday 4:30-6:00 PM plus appointments) and email-first communication suggest a tight-knit, member-focused operation rather than a high-volume retail banking model.

The credit union's limited online presence and information availability present challenges for prospective members seeking detailed product comparisons. Operating hours are significantly restricted compared to traditional banks or larger credit unions, which may inconvenience members with standard business-hour availability. The reliance on Gmail for member communication and GoDaddy-hosted website suggests minimal investment in digital infrastructure, which could impact member experience and data security perception.
services: Member financial counseling (implicit in 'help with financial problems')
pros: NCUA insured deposits up to $250,000 for member protection
Established 57-year track record (since 1967) serving Chicago community
Union-focused mission with documented support during five work stoppages
Quick and easy loan approval process for members
Flexible loan purposes including debt consolidation, emergencies, and home improvement
Payroll deduction options available for loan repayment
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure with earnings returned to members
cons: Severely limited operating hours (Monday and Thursday 4:30-6:00 PM only, plus appointments)
Minimal online banking infrastructure and digital services presence
Generic Gmail address (cucu1600@gmail.com) raises professional communication concerns
Very limited product information and rates details publicly available on website
No apparent mobile app, online account access, or digital loan application system
best_for: Union members and employees at Chicago-area colleges and universities
Chicago residents seeking union-friendly financial services with mission-driven values
Members needing debt consolidation or emergency loans with flexible approval
Savers prioritizing NCUA insurance protection and member-owned financial institutions
diagnosis: College and University Credit Union is best for Chicago-area union members and college-affiliated individuals seeking a mission-driven, member-owned alternative to traditional banks with NCUA-protected savings and personal loans. The primary caveat is the severe operational constraint: extremely limited hours and minimal digital infrastructure make this impractical for most consumers unless location and union affiliation create strong alignment with the institution's community.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: College and University — NCUA-insured credit union in Chicago, IL. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
